task.prompt: A request-scoped provider using useFactory is silently injected as null when the same consumer also injects a TRANSIENT provider and the application has multiple global modules. The dependency tree staticness check is called before all provider wiring is complete, caches an incorrect true result, and is never invalidated. Later when the request context is resolved, the cached result causes the resolver to short-circuit and return the pre-existing static instance which is null for a request-scoped provider. No error is thrown and the provider arrives as null in the constructor. Investigate how the dependency tree staticness is evaluated and cached and fix the premature caching.

hint_levels.symptom_only: A request-scoped provider injected via useFactory arrives as null in its consumer's constructor under specific module configurations. The injection silently fails with no error, no undefined, and no DI exception. Removing other providers or changing the factory to useClass works around the issue.
hint_levels.file_hint: The bug is in packages/core/injector/instance-wrapper.ts in isDependencyTreeStatic at lines 330-348. The isTreeStatic boolean is cached permanently on the first call. If called before all dependencies are wired into INSTANCE_METADATA_SYMBOL.dependencies, the cached true value is never invalidated. In abstract-instance-resolver.ts at lines 60-77, the cached true value causes resolvePerContext to return the null static instance instead of resolving through context.
hint_levels.function_hint: In instance-wrapper.ts isDependencyTreeStatic at line 332, the early return on cached isTreeStatic prevents re-evaluation. In abstract-instance-resolver.ts resolvePerContext at line 62, when isDependencyTreeStatic() returns true the method returns wrapperRef.instance which is null for request-scoped providers not yet resolved in this context. The fix is to not permanently cache isTreeStatic when the dependency metadata may be incomplete, or to add a null guard in resolvePerContext before returning the static instance.

reasoning_chain: Read symptom: request-scoped useFactory provider is null in constructor with no DI error
Open instance-wrapper.ts and find isDependencyTreeStatic at lines 330-348
Find isTreeStatic caching: line 332 early-returns the cached value, line 341 permanently stores the computed result
Open abstract-instance-resolver.ts and find resolvePerContext at lines 60-77
Find line 62: if isDependencyTreeStatic() returns true, wrapperRef.instance is returned directly — this is null for unresolved request-scoped providers
Open instance-loader.ts to understand the order loadInstance is called and when metadata is wired
Open injector.ts loadInstance at lines 127-200 to see when resolveProperties populates dependency metadata
Open module.ts addProvider at lines 278-318 to confirm InstanceWrapper starts with no deps
Open scanner.ts insertProvider at lines 381-431 to understand registration order with global modules
Apply fix: do not permanently cache isTreeStatic when called before full wiring, or add null guard in resolvePerContext before returning static instance
